rationale: A is better anchored to Flink 2.2.0 with exact runtime class and dedicated test filename, and it explicitly notes uncertainty about behavior. B is vaguer, omits the concrete neighboring test, and adds speculative test cases and semantics not grounded in source artifacts.

A: Includes a broader state_backends doc that is only loosely related to checkpoint ID allocation.
B: Does not name the concrete neighboring test class, only the package.
Suggests speculative behaviors and tests (thread-safety, shutdown semantics) without source grounding.
Fails to anchor the answer as specifically to the release-2.2.0 source layout.
